text: Iran internal split now visible: Pezeshkian tells European Council he has 'necessary will to end war' if guarantees met. But Mojtaba Khamenei contradicts: 'Hormuz leverage must continue, demand is continuation of defense.' IRGC silent on talks. Power struggle between elected president and supreme leader playing out in real time.
verificationNote: CNBC, France 24, Al Jazeera confirmed both positions.

text: Oil prices crashing on Trump exit signals: Brent down from $108 AM to ~$102 PM. WTI dipped below $100. Goldman estimates $14-18/bbl geopolitical premium baked into current prices. May Brent expired at record $118.35. But IEA chief Birol warns 'April will be much worse than March' for actual physical supply.
verificationNote: CNBC, Fortune, Bloomberg confirmed price movements.

text: Trump confirms primetime address at 9PM ET tonight — first major address on Iran war. Will reaffirm 2-3 week timeline. But bigger news: told Reuters he is 'absolutely' considering pulling US out of NATO over allies' refusal to join war. This would fundamentally reshape the global security order.
verificationNote: NPR, NBC News, Washington Post confirmed.

text: Hezbollah Southern Front commander Hajj Youssef Ismail Hashem killed in IDF naval strike south of Beirut. This follows 46 attack waves on March 31 — double the previous day. Despite losses, Hezbollah sustaining 29 rocket/missile + 17 UAV attacks per day. IDF also struck fentanyl factory it claims was tied to Iran chemical weapons program.
verificationNote: Alma Center daily report, Times of Israel confirmed.

text: Pope Leo XIV calls for Easter ceasefire: 'I hope Trump is looking for an off-ramp.' Vatican says war fails just war criteria. Britain hosting 35-nation Hormuz meeting tomorrow. Australian PM Albanese addresses nation on economic shocks. International pressure for ceasefire mounting from all directions.
verificationNote: Manila Times, Washington Post, CNN confirmed.

text: US equipment losses growing per Axios: 12 MQ-9 Reapers ($192-678M), 1 E-3 AWACS (~$500M, irreplaceable until 2030s), 4 THAAD radars ($2.7B), 3 F-15Es to friendly fire, 1 F-35 damaged, 850+ Tomahawks expended. These are not trivial losses — the E-3 and THAAD radars represent capability gaps that won't be filled for years.

Day 33 PM summary (10:35 AM PDT): (1) Oil crashing — Brent ~$102/WTI ~$99; (2) Trump primetime 9PM + NATO exit threat; (3) Pezeshkian ceasefire vs Khamenei resistance split; (4) Pope Easter ceasefire; (5) IEA 'April worse'; (6) Britain 35-nation Hormuz meeting; (7) Hezbollah commander killed; (8) Fentanyl factory struck; (9) Zanjan 4 killed/mosque; (10) Equipment losses mounting.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, NPR, NBC News, France 24, Alma Center, Times of Israel, Axios.
